name: sidecar-ai
description: Autonomous AI agent for coding — run full agent loops with local Ollama, Anthropic Claude, or OpenAI models

sidecar.baseUrl.markdownDescription:
Base URL for the active LLM backend.

| Backend | URL |
|---|---|
| Ollama (local) | `http://localhost:11434` |
| Anthropic | `https://api.anthropic.com` |
| OpenAI | `https://api.openai.com` |
| Kickstand *(coming soon)* | `http://localhost:11435` |

Switch backends the easy way: `SideCar: Switch Backend` in the Command Palette.

sidecar.apiKey.markdownDescription:
API key for the active backend. **Stored in VS Code SecretStorage** — never in `settings.json`. Run `SideCar: Set / Refresh API Key` to paste or rotate your key securely. Plaintext values in this setting are migrated to SecretStorage on activation and then cleared.

Ignored for local Ollama; required for Anthropic and OpenAI.

sidecar.model.markdownDescription:
Model to use for chat. Examples:

- Ollama: `qwen3-coder:30b`, `deepseek-coder:33b`, `llama3.2:latest`
- Anthropic: `claude-opus-4-6`, `claude-sonnet-4-5`, `claude-haiku-4-5`
- OpenAI: `gpt-5`, `gpt-4o`, `gpt-4o-mini`

Pick keyboard-first with `SideCar: Select Model`.

enumDescriptions: Detect from the base URL: localhost:11434 → Ollama, anthropic.com → Anthropic, openrouter.ai → OpenRouter, groq.com → Groq, fireworks.ai → Fireworks, localhost:11435 → Kickstand (coming soon), anything else → OpenAI-compatible.
Local Ollama (http://localhost:11434). Free, private, no key needed.
Anthropic Claude API. Requires an API key stored via `SideCar: Set / Refresh API Key`.
OpenAI-compatible API (OpenAI, LM Studio, vLLM, llama.cpp, together.ai). Requires an API key.

OpenRouter (https://openrouter.ai/api/v1). One API key unlocks hundreds of models across providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Mistral, Meta, and more) with per-model pricing pulled live from their catalog. Requires an OpenRouter API key.

Groq (https://api.groq.com/openai/v1). LPU inference serves open-weight models (Llama 3.3, Mixtral, DeepSeek R1 distills) at thousands of tokens/sec. Free tier with rate limits. Requires a Groq API key from console.groq.com.
Fireworks (https://api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1). Hosts open-weight models like DeepSeek V3, Qwen 2.5 Coder, Llama 3.3, Mixtral at OpenAI-compatible pricing. Requires an API key from fireworks.ai.

Kickstand self-hosted LLM server (http://localhost:11435). Coming soon — not yet officially released; the backend adapter ships today for anyone running a local dev build. Reads its token from `~/.config/kickstand/token`.
sidecar.provider.markdownDescription: Which backend adapter to use when talking to the LLM. Leave as `auto` unless you're running an OpenAI-compatible server at a non-standard URL that the auto-detector can't recognize.

sidecar.fallbackBaseUrl.markdownDescription:
Fallback backend URL. If the primary backend fails repeatedly, SideCar switches to this URL automatically. Leave empty to disable fallback.

Typical use: primary = Anthropic, fallback = local Ollama, so a transient API outage doesn't block your work.

sidecar.fallbackApiKey.markdownDescription: API key for the fallback backend. **Stored in VS Code SecretStorage** — plaintext values are migrated on activation and then cleared from `settings.json`.

sidecar.fallbackModel.markdownDescription: Model to use on the fallback backend. Empty string = reuse the primary model (only useful when the primary and fallback are the same provider family).

sidecar.requestTimeout.description: Timeout in seconds for each LLM request. If no tokens arrive within this time the request is aborted. Set to 0 to disable.

sidecar.dailyBudget.markdownDescription: Daily spending budget in USD for paid backends. Agent runs are blocked when the limit is reached. `0` = disabled. Check current spend with `SideCar: Show Session Spend`.

sidecar.weeklyBudget.markdownDescription: Weekly spending budget in USD for paid backends. Agent runs are blocked when the limit is reached. `0` = disabled.

sidecar.modelRouting.enabled.markdownDescription: Route each agent dispatch role (`chat`, `agent-loop`, `completion`, `summarize`, `critic`, `worker`, `planner`, `judge`, `visual`, `embed`) to a rule-selected model. Opt-in — when `false`, every role uses `#sidecar.model#` (legacy behavior). Configure rules via `#sidecar.modelRouting.rules#`.

model.description: Model id to dispatch with when this rule matches.

fallbackModel.description: Downgrade target when a budget cap on this rule is exceeded (v0.64 phase 4c).

sessionBudget.description: Session spend cap in USD. Budget tripped → downgrade to `fallbackModel`.

sidecar.modelRouting.rules.markdownDescription: Ordered list of routing rules, first match wins. See `#sidecar.modelRouting.enabled#` for the opt-in switch. Example: `[{ "when": "agent-loop.complexity=high", "model": "claude-opus-4-6" }, { "when": "summarize", "model": "claude-haiku-4-5" }, { "when": "chat", "model": "ollama/llama3:70b" }]`

sidecar.modelRouting.defaultModel.markdownDescription: Model used when no rule in `#sidecar.modelRouting.rules#` matches. Empty → falls back to `#sidecar.model#`.

sidecar.modelRouting.visibleSwaps.markdownDescription: Show a brief toast whenever a routing rule swaps the active model mid-session. Turn off once you've calibrated your rules and don't want to see the notifications.

sidecar.modelRouting.dryRun.markdownDescription: Log what the router *would* have selected but dispatch using `#sidecar.model#` anyway. Useful for safely calibrating rules before turning `#sidecar.modelRouting.enabled#` on.

sidecar.agentMode.markdownDescription:
How much autonomy the agent has. Built-in modes:

- `cautious` — asks before destructive tools (default)
- `autonomous` — runs every allowed tool without asking
- `manual` — asks before every tool
- `plan` — generates a plan for your approval, then executes
- `review` — queues file edits for review; nothing hits disk
- `audit` *(v0.60)* — agent runs without per-call prompts, but every `write_file` / `edit_file` / `delete_file` is diverted to an in-memory buffer instead of touching disk. The user reviews the buffered changes at the end and either accepts (atomic flush) or rejects (discard). Shell commands still run normally — scope is the agent's explicit file-authoring surface, not every side effect.

Custom modes defined in `#sidecar.customModes#` are also valid here.

sidecar.agentTemperature.markdownDescription:
Temperature for agent tool-calling requests.

- `0.1-0.3` — deterministic tool selection (recommended for agent loops)
- `0.7-1.0` — creative, more exploration

Non-tool requests always use `0`.

sidecar.agentMaxIterations.markdownDescription: Maximum number of agent loop iterations before the agent stops. Each iteration is one model call plus any tools it chose. Hitting this limit usually means the agent is stuck in a cycle — inspect the chat and narrow the task.

sidecar.agentMaxMessages.markdownDescription: Soft ceiling on conversation message count before the agent should wrap up. The agent still completes the current tool cycle if it's in the middle of one.

sidecar.agentMaxTokens.markdownDescription: Maximum total tokens consumed by a single agent run before it stops. Prevents runaway loops on paid backends. `100000` is a safe default for Claude; bump higher for frontier models with large context windows.

sidecar.systemPrompt.markdownDescription: System prompt override. Leave empty to use SideCar's built-in prompt (recommended). Custom prompts compose on top of the base; they don't replace it.

sidecar.bgMaxConcurrent.description: Maximum number of background agents that can run simultaneously

sidecar.shellTimeout.description: Default timeout for shell commands in seconds

sidecar.shellMaxOutputMB.description: Maximum shell output size in MB before truncation

sidecar.critic.enabled.description: Run an adversarial critic LLM call after every write_file / edit_file and after every failed run_tests. High-severity findings block the agent until addressed; low-severity findings surface as chat annotations only. Doubles API spend on edit turns — off by default.

sidecar.critic.model.description: Model to use for the adversarial critic call. Empty string (default) means reuse sidecar.model. Set to a cheaper / faster model (e.g. claude-haiku-4-5 when the main model is claude-opus-4-6) to cut cost while keeping the critic enabled.

sidecar.critic.blockOnHighSeverity.description: When the critic reports a high-severity finding, inject a synthetic user message forcing the agent to address it before the turn can finish. When false, high-severity findings surface as chat annotations only (same as low-severity).

sidecar.autoFixOnFailure.description: Automatically check for errors after agent edits and feed them back to the model for self-correction

sidecar.completionGate.enabled.description: Require the agent to run lint and tests for files it edited before declaring completion. Catches cases where the agent reports a change as done without verifying.

sidecar.steerQueue.coalesceWindowMs.description: When a user steer is queued, wait up to this many ms at the next iteration boundary to collect further submissions into a single coalesced turn. 0 disables coalescing (drain immediately).

sidecar.steerQueue.maxPending.description: Maximum number of queued steers. When full, the oldest nudge is dropped to make room; an all-interrupt queue rejects new submissions.

sidecar.multiFileEdits.enabled.description: Enable multi-file edit streams: the agent plans an edit DAG before executing batches of file writes, then dispatches independent writes in parallel.

sidecar.multiFileEdits.maxParallel.description: Maximum number of file writes dispatched concurrently during a multi-file edit.

sidecar.multiFileEdits.planningPass.description: Run a dedicated Edit Plan LLM turn before executing multi-file writes. Adds one extra turn of latency; disable to let the agent emit writes directly (the DAG scheduler still applies).

sidecar.multiFileEdits.minFilesForPlan.description: Minimum number of file writes in one turn that triggers the Edit Plan pass. Smaller edits go directly to execution.

sidecar.multiFileEdits.plannerModel.description: Override model used for the Edit Plan turn. Empty = reuse the main model. Point to a smaller local model (e.g. qwen2.5-coder:7b) to keep planning cheap.

sidecar.multiFileEdits.reviewGranularity.description: How the Pending Changes panel presents a multi-file edit. 'bulk' = accept/reject all as one unit, 'per-file' = checkboxes per file (default), 'per-hunk' = drill down to hunk-level across files.

sidecar.retrieval.graphExpansion.enabled.description: After semantic retrieval returns vector hits, walk the symbol graph's 'calls' edges outward by 1-2 hops (depth auto-scales with the model's context window) to surface dependency-coupled symbols that wouldn't score on keywords alone. Matters for dense, deeply-interconnected codebases (physics simulations, signal processing, transform libraries).

sidecar.retrieval.graphExpansion.maxHits.description: Cap on symbols added via graph walk per retrieval call. Higher = deeper dependency coverage at more token cost; lower = tighter context for small-context local models.

sidecar.facets.enabled.description: Enable Typed Sub-Agent Facets — specialized sub-agents declared in .sidecar/facets/*.md with typed tool allowlists and RPC schemas. Built-in facets (general-coder, latex-writer, signal-processing, frontend, test-author, technical-writer, security-reviewer, data-engineer) are always available.

sidecar.facets.maxConcurrent.description: Maximum number of facets running in parallel. Guards GPU and context pressure when several specialists share the same local model. Within a single dispatch, independent facets run concurrently up to this cap; dependent facets wait for their prerequisites regardless.

sidecar.facets.rpcTimeoutMs.description: Timeout for a single typed RPC call between facets. Exceeded calls return a synthetic timeout error to the caller rather than hanging the dispatch.

sidecar.facets.registry.description: Absolute paths to additional facet .md files (merged with built-ins). Omit to use only the built-in catalog plus any facets at .sidecar/facets/*.md in the active workspace.

sidecar.sidecarMd.mode.markdownDescription:
How SIDECAR.md content is injected into the system prompt.

| Mode | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `sections` (default) | Parse H2 boundaries, route sections to the system prompt based on `<!-- @paths: glob -->` sentinels, active editor, and user-mentioned paths. Sections without a sentinel default to always-included. Drops whole sections on overflow instead of mid-chopping. Degrades to `full` when no section declares a sentinel. |
| `full` | Legacy behavior — dump the entire file and mid-chop on overflow. Use this if you've customized your SIDECAR.md in ways the parser doesn't handle cleanly. |

Path-scoped sections declare their globs via an HTML-comment sentinel immediately under the H2 heading (invisible in GitHub's markdown preview):

```markdown
## Transforms
<!-- @paths: src/transforms/**, src/dsp/** -->
Filter kernels go under src/transforms/...
```

sidecar.sidecarMd.alwaysIncludeHeadings.description: H2 headings that always get included in the system prompt regardless of @paths sentinels. Matched case-insensitively against section.heading. Useful for teams that don't want to edit their SIDECAR.md — 'always include the Build section' lives in user settings, not in the doc.

sidecar.sidecarMd.lowPriorityHeadings.description: H2 headings that are demoted to low priority — included only when budget remains after always + scoped sections. Matched case-insensitively.

sidecar.sidecarMd.maxScopedSections.description: Cap on how many path-scoped sections can land in one injection. Guards against a wildcard-ish path glob that matches 30 sections.

sidecar.fork.enabled.description: Enable Fork & Parallel Solve — the /fork command spawns N parallel approaches to the same task, each in its own Shadow Workspace, and presents them side-by-side for comparison.

sidecar.fork.defaultCount.description: Default number of forks spawned by /fork when no explicit count is passed. Clamped 2–10; values outside that range degenerate to 'agent loop with no comparison.'

sidecar.fork.maxConcurrent.description: Max forks running in parallel at once. Clamped to [1, numForks] per dispatch. Higher values finish faster at the cost of more concurrent Shadow Workspaces (disk churn) and more concurrent LLM requests (cost + rate limits).

sidecar.regressionGuards.markdownDescription:
Declarative shell commands that act as hard gates the agent must pass before completing a task. Use for domain-specific correctness checks the general lint/test suite can't express — conservation-of-energy invariants in a physics sim, bundle-size budgets, API-contract diffs, etc.

Each entry has: `name` (required, human-readable), `command` (required, shell), `trigger` (required, one of `post-write` / `post-turn` / `pre-completion`), and optional `blocking` (default `true`), `timeoutMs` (default 30000), `scope` (glob array; guard only fires when touched files match), `maxAttempts` (default 5 consecutive failures before giving up), `workingDir`.

When blocking and exit ≠ 0, the guard output is injected as a synthetic user turn so the agent can read the error and revise. When non-blocking, the loop just surfaces a warning. The first time a workspace defines guards, SideCar prompts to trust them — same contract as `hooks`, `mcpServers`, `customTools`, `scheduledTasks`.

name.description: Short identifier for the guard (shown in failure messages).

command.description: Shell command to run. Runs in the workspace root unless workingDir is set.

trigger.description: When the guard fires. post-write = after every turn that includes a file-mutation tool call; post-turn = after every turn; pre-completion = when the model tries to finish the task.

blocking.description: When true, failure injects a synthetic user turn so the agent must address it. When false, failure surfaces as a warning and the loop continues.

timeoutMs.description: Hard timeout for the command in ms.

scope.description: Optional glob list. Guard only fires when at least one touched file in this turn matches. Empty/missing = fire on every qualifying turn.

maxAttempts.description: Give up after this many consecutive failures per task. Counter resets on any success.

workingDir.description: Optional working directory. Defaults to the workspace folder.

sidecar.regressionGuards.mode.markdownDescription:
Global override for `sidecar.regressionGuards`.

- `strict` (default) — honor each guard's per-entry `blocking` flag.
- `warn` — force every guard to non-blocking, so failures surface as warnings but don't halt the agent. Useful for short-term opt-out during a known-broken refactor without editing every entry.
- `off` — disable all guards regardless of config. Useful for emergency bypass.

sidecar.audit.autoApproveReads.markdownDescription: *(Audit Mode, v0.60)* When true (default), `read_file` / `list_directory` calls don't need to go through the audit buffer review — reads don't mutate state, so they pass through even when the agent is running in `audit` mode. Set to `false` to require explicit approval for every read (rarely useful; mostly for auditing sessions where the set of files consulted is itself sensitive).

sidecar.audit.bufferGitCommits.markdownDescription: *(Audit Mode, v0.60)* When true (default), `git_commit` tool calls are buffered alongside file writes so a rejected audit batch leaves `HEAD` unchanged. When false, commits land immediately regardless of audit state — useful for session-tracking commits (changelog entries, audit trail) the user wants to keep even if the code changes are rejected. v0.61 a.4 wires the behavior end-to-end: queued commits execute in FIFO order as the last step of a full flush; subset flushes leave commits queued.

sidecar.pr.create.draftByDefault.markdownDescription: *(Draft PR, v0.68)* When true (default), `SideCar: Create Pull Request` opens PRs as drafts — so CI runs but no reviewer is pinged until you mark ready. Set false to open a ready-for-review PR on the first push.

sidecar.pr.create.baseBranch.markdownDescription: *(Draft PR, v0.68)* Base branch for new PRs. `auto` (default) resolves the remote's default branch via `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD`, falling back to `main`. Set to an explicit branch name (e.g. `develop`) to override for repos that target a non-default base.

sidecar.pr.create.template.markdownDescription: *(Draft PR, v0.68)* How to handle repo PR templates. `auto` (default) reads `.github/pull_request_template.md` or `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` and asks the model to fill each section. `ignore` writes a fresh Summary + Test Plan body regardless. Any other value is treated as an absolute/relative path to a custom template file.

sidecar.includeWorkspace.markdownDescription: Automatically include workspace files in chat context. When off, only files you explicitly reference with `@file:path` or pin with `@pin:path` are sent to the model.

sidecar.includeActiveFile.description: Include the currently active file in chat context

sidecar.filePatterns.markdownDescription: Glob patterns for files eligible to be included as workspace context. Applied before `sidecar.maxFiles` ranks them by relevance. Patterns use standard VS Code globbing (`**`, `*`, `?`).

sidecar.maxFiles.markdownDescription: Maximum number of workspace files included as context on each turn. Lower this if you're hitting context-limit warnings on a small local model; raise it for frontier models with larger windows.

sidecar.contextLimit.markdownDescription: Override the context token limit for local models. `0` = auto-detect with a 16K default cap. Increase if you have enough VRAM and want longer conversations. Ignored for paid backends — their limits come from the API.

sidecar.pinnedContext.description: Files or folders always included in context (relative paths). Use @pin:path in chat to add dynamically.

sidecar.projectKnowledge.enabled.markdownDescription: *(Project Knowledge Index, v0.61+)* Symbol-level semantic index. SideCar embeds each function / class / method / interface / type as its own vector (vs. the file-level index's one-vector-per-file) so queries like "where is auth handled?" return the specific function rather than the containing file. Uses the existing `@xenova/transformers` MiniLM model — no new downloads, no new dependencies. **Default `true` as of v0.63.** Existing users who set this to `false` explicitly keep their setting; new installs index symbols automatically. First activation re-embeds the workspace (~5–10s on a typical repo); subsequent activations replay the cached index. Disable by setting to `false` if the startup cost bites on very large monorepos (>100k symbols) — the file-level index still works as a fallback.

sidecar.projectKnowledge.maxSymbolsPerFi.markdownDescription: *(Project Knowledge Index, v0.61)* Safety cap on how many symbols from a single file get embedded. Guards against generated files (e.g. auto-generated schemas, giant test fixtures) with tens of thousands of declarations monopolizing the embedder.

sidecar.projectKnowledge.backend.markdownDescription:
*(Project Knowledge Index, v0.62)* Vector storage backend for the symbol embedding index.

- `flat` (default, shipped) — in-memory `Float32Array` with linear cosine-scan search. Fast enough for workspaces up to ~100k symbols.
- `lance` — reserved for a future release with LanceDB-backed HNSW ANN. Selecting it today logs a warning and falls back to `flat`.

The backend is swappable without rebuilding the index — both implementations read the same on-disk format.

sidecar.merkleIndex.enabled.markdownDescription:
*(Project Knowledge Index — Merkle layer, v0.62)* Content-addressed hash tree over the symbol embedding index. When on + `sidecar.projectKnowledge.enabled` is also on, every symbol mutation mirrors into a Merkle tree and `project_knowledge_search` prunes candidate files via aggregated-embedding descent *before* scoring leaves — turns an O(total symbols) cosine scan into O(picked files × symbols per file).

Disable only when debugging retrieval regressions: a quality drop against the RAG-eval baseline while this is on isolates the issue to the Merkle layer without losing PKI entirely. The root hash is also exposed (via `getMerkleRoot()` on the index) as a single-value workspace fingerprint for cache validity + cross-machine sync work.

sidecar.skills.userRegistry.markdownDescription: *(Skill Sync — v0.64)* Git URL (or absolute local folder) containing your personal skill collection. On activation, SideCar clones or pulls it into `~/.sidecar/user-skills/` and the SkillLoader picks up every `.agent.md` inside as a user-scope skill. Empty (default) → disabled. Standard git auth applies — SSH keys / gh auth / HTTPS tokens all work.

sidecar.skills.teamRegistries.markdownDescription: *(Skill Sync — v0.64)* Array of git URLs, each cloned into `~/.sidecar/team-skills/<slug>/`. Useful when you belong to multiple teams with overlapping skill collections — the picker tags each skill by its origin registry so you can resolve name collisions deterministically.

sidecar.skills.autoPull.markdownDescription: *(Skill Sync — v0.64)* When to pull configured registries. `on-start` refreshes on every activation (cheap for small skill repos). `manual` only syncs when you run the `SideCar: Sync Skill Registries` command.

sidecar.skills.trustedRegistries.markdownDescription: *(Skill Sync — v0.64)* Registry URLs that skip the first-install trust prompt. Skills from trusted registries still respect per-skill `allowed-tools` / `disable-model-invocation` guardrails; this setting only waives the initial consent modal.

sidecar.skills.offline.markdownDescription: *(Skill Sync — v0.64)* Air-gapped mode. When `true`, SideCar makes no network calls for skill sync — the SkillLoader reads only from what's already cached in `~/.sidecar/{user,team}-skills/`. Useful for restrictive CI environments or when working offline.

sidecar.promptPruning.enabled.markdownDescription:
Prune prompts before sending to paid backends (Anthropic, OpenAI). Collapses whitespace, truncates oversized tool results, and dedupes repeated file content. **Safe for agent loops** — only lossy on tool output, never on user or assistant messages.

Check the savings in `SideCar: Show Session Spend` after each run.

sidecar.promptPruning.maxToolResultToken.markdownDescription: Maximum token count for any single `tool_result` block sent to a paid backend. Longer results are head+tail truncated with an elision marker. Raise this if you need the agent to see full file contents; lower it to reduce cost on heavy exploration tasks.

sidecar.jsDocSync.enabled.description: On save, check TypeScript / JavaScript files for JSDoc @param tags that no longer match the function signature. Stale tags are surfaced as diagnostics with quick-fix actions to add, remove, or rename them.

sidecar.readmeSync.enabled.description: On save and open, check fenced code blocks in README.md for calls to workspace-exported functions whose argument count no longer matches the current signature. Stale calls are surfaced as diagnostics with a quick-fix that rewrites the arguments.

sidecar.shadowWorkspace.mode.markdownDescription:
Controls the Shadow Workspace feature — a git-worktree sandbox at `.sidecar/shadows/<task-id>/` where the agent iterates without touching the main working tree. On task completion, the user sees a diff and can accept (apply to main) or reject (discard).

- `off` (default) — disabled. Agent writes land directly in main.
- `opt-in` — agent tasks run in the main tree; the user can opt a specific task into a shadow via a slash command. *(Not yet wired up in v0.59 MVP.)*
- `always` — every agent task runs in a shadow. Safe but introduces per-task setup cost (worktree checkout) and a review step at end.

This is the v0.59 MVP tier. Per-hunk review UI, gate-command integration, symlinked build dirs, and rebase-on-moved-main conflict handling land in v0.60+.

sidecar.shadowWorkspace.autoCleanup.markdownDescription: When true, `git worktree remove` + directory cleanup run after every shadow regardless of accept/reject. Set to `false` to keep failed or rejected shadows around for post-mortem inspection at `.sidecar/shadows/<task-id>/` — useful when debugging why the agent's shadow work didn't apply cleanly.

sidecar.shadowWorkspace.gateCommand.markdownDescription:
Shell command run inside the shadow before the accept/reject prompt opens, so failing builds can't ship as accepted diffs. Defaults to `npm run check` which runs tsc + eslint + vitest per the SideCar project's own convention. Override for non-JS projects (e.g. `cargo check && cargo test`, `pytest && mypy`).

**Not yet wired up in v0.59 MVP** — the setting exists so the gate lands zero-config in v0.60.

sidecar.shadowWorkspace.sweepStaleOnActi.markdownDescription:
*(v0.62.1)* Sweep orphan shadow worktrees at activation. A VS Code crash mid-shadow-run can leave git metadata under `.git/worktrees/<id>/` pointing at a directory that no longer exists — downstream `git status` / `git fetch` / `git push` start failing mysteriously. When enabled (default), SideCar runs `git worktree remove --force` on any stale entry whose shadow path is gone + deletes orphan dirs under `.sidecar/shadows/` that aren't registered with git.

Disable when doing crash-recovery forensics on your own shadows; the manual `SideCar: Shadow Workspaces: Sweep Stale Worktrees` command stays available regardless of this setting.

sidecar.terminalExecution.enabled.markdownDescription: Route the agent's `run_command` tool through VS Code's shell-integration API so the user sees commands execute in a dedicated *SideCar Agent* terminal (as opposed to the hidden `child_process.spawn` path `ShellSession` used previously). Gives transparency on SSH / Dev Containers / WSL / Codespaces where `child_process.exec` escapes the remote sandbox. Set to `false` to keep everything on `ShellSession`.

sidecar.terminalExecution.terminalName.markdownDescription: Display name for the reusable agent terminal when `terminalExecution.enabled` is true.

sidecar.terminalExecution.fallbackToChil.markdownDescription: When shell integration is unavailable (bare shell without the VS Code init script, older VS Code version), fall back to the `ShellSession` / `child_process` execution path. Set to `false` to make commands fail loudly instead of silently falling back — useful for debugging terminal-integration setup issues.

sidecar.terminalExecution.shellIntegrati.markdownDescription: Milliseconds to wait for `shellIntegration` to attach to a freshly created terminal before falling back to `ShellSession`. Shell integration attaches asynchronously after the terminal's shell loads VS Code's init script, so a short wait is normal. Raise if your shell startup is slow; lower to fail faster on shells that won't ever integrate.

sidecar.terminalErrorInterception.description: Detect non-zero exit codes in the integrated terminal and offer to diagnose the failure in the SideCar chat. Requires VS Code shell integration.

sidecar.completionModel.description: Model for inline completions (leave empty to use the chat model)

sidecar.completionMaxTokens.description: Maximum tokens for inline completion responses

sidecar.completionDebounceMs.description: Minimum interval in milliseconds between completion requests

enumDescriptions: Tighter message padding and gaps — fits more messages on screen, good for side-by-side layouts.
The default — balanced padding matching VS Code's overall density.
Relaxed padding and larger gaps — easier reading on high-DPI displays.
sidecar.chatDensity.markdownDescription: Chat panel density. Affects message padding, avatar size, and vertical rhythm — not the font size (use `#sidecar.chatFontSize#` for that).

sidecar.chatFontSize.markdownDescription: Chat UI base font size in pixels. Range: 10–22.

sidecar.chatAccentColor.markdownDescription: Override the chat accent color used for user messages and step indicators. Any valid CSS color (`#ffbb00`, `hsl(200 80% 50%)`, `royalblue`). Leave empty to inherit from the active VS Code theme.

sidecar.enableMermaid.markdownDescription: Render fenced ` ```mermaid ` code blocks as diagrams in the chat. Disable to skip the 5.2MB mermaid.js load and render them as plain code — useful if you never ask for diagrams and want a smaller runtime footprint.

sidecar.expandThinking.markdownDescription: Show model reasoning (`<thinking>`) blocks expanded by default instead of collapsed. Useful when watching how frontier models reason, noisy for everyday chat.

sidecar.verboseMode.markdownDescription: Show detailed agent reasoning in the chat — iteration summaries, tool selection context, and system prompt assembly. Turn off for a quieter UI at the cost of debuggability.

sidecar.mcpServers.markdownDescription:
MCP servers to connect to. Supports stdio, HTTP, and SSE transports. Also reads `.mcp.json` at workspace root.

**stdio:** `{ "command": "npx", "args": ["server"] }`

**HTTP:** `{ "type": "http", "url": "https://...", "headers": {} }`

**Options:** `tools` (per-tool enable/disable), `maxResultChars` (output limit, default 50000)

sidecar.customTools.description: Custom tools that run shell commands. Format: [{ "name": "...", "description": "...", "command": "..." }]

sidecar.customModes.description: Custom agent modes with dedicated system prompts and approval behavior. Example: [{ "name": "architect", "description": "Design-only mode", "systemPrompt": "Focus on architecture...", "approvalBehavior": "manual" }]

description.description: Short description shown in the mode dropdown

systemPrompt.description: Additional system prompt text injected when this mode is active

approvalBehavior.description: Tool approval behavior for this mode

toolPermissions.description: Per-tool permission overrides for this mode

sidecar.hooks.description: Pre/post-execution hooks by tool name. Keys are tool names or '*' for all. Value: { "pre": "command", "post": "command" }

sidecar.scheduledTasks.description: Recurring tasks to run on interval

sidecar.delegateTask.enabled.markdownDescription: Expose the `delegate_task` tool to paid backends. The orchestrator can offload read-only research (file reads, greps, searches) to a local Ollama worker and receive a compact summary, cutting paid-model token usage on heavy exploration tasks. **No-op on local-only setups.**

sidecar.delegateTask.workerModel.markdownDescription: Ollama model used by the `delegate_task` worker. Empty string = reuse the chat model. Recommended: a code-tuned model like `qwen3-coder:30b` or `deepseek-coder:33b`.

sidecar.delegateTask.workerBaseUrl.markdownDescription: Base URL of the Ollama instance the `delegate_task` worker connects to. Must point at a local or reachable Ollama server — never Anthropic or OpenAI (defeats the purpose).

sidecar.delegateTask.maxIterations.markdownDescription: Hard cap on agent iterations for a delegated worker run. Workers are scoped to focused read-only research, so a tight cap protects against runaway loops and unintended cost. Raise this only if your delegated tasks legitimately need more steps (e.g. deep multi-file analysis). The main agent loop is bounded separately by `#sidecar.agentMaxIterations#`.

sidecar.outboundAllowlist.markdownDescription:
Outbound host allowlist for URL fetching in chat.

When non-empty, SideCar only fetches URLs whose hostname matches one of these entries. Empty (the default) allows every public URL — SSRF and private-IP blocking still apply.

Patterns support a leading `*.` wildcard for subdomains:

- `github.com` — exact match only
- `*.github.com` — any subdomain (`api.github.com`, `raw.github.com`)

Pairs with the built-in exfiltration defense on `web_search`, which refuses to send queries containing credential-shaped tokens regardless of this setting.

sidecar.fetchUrlContext.markdownDescription: Automatically fetch web-page content when URLs are pasted into chat. The fetched HTML is converted to markdown and attached as context on the next turn.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with SideCar
walkthroughs.description: Local-first AI coding assistant — chat, inline edits, code actions, and native Problems integration.

steps.title: Welcome
steps.description: What SideCar is and what ships in the box.

steps.title: Pick a backend
steps.description: Run locally with Ollama, or plug in an Anthropic / OpenAI key. Keys are stored in SecretStorage.

steps.title: Open the chat
steps.description: Toggle the SideCar chat sidebar and send your first message.

steps.title: Inline editing and the lightbulb
steps.description: Inline chat with ⌘I, and Fix / Explain / Refactor from the code actions menu.

steps.description: Browse the Command Palette, Problems panel integration, and pending-change review.

scripts.copy-grammars: mkdir -p grammars && cp node_modules/web-tree-sitter/tree-sitter.wasm grammars/ && cp node_modules/tree-sitter-wasms/out/tree-sitter-typescript.wasm node_modules/tree-sitter-wasms/out/tree-sitter-tsx.wasm node_modules/tree-sitter-wasms/out/tree-sitter-javascript.wasm node_modules/tree-sitter-wasms/out/tree-sitter-python.wasm node_modules/tree-sitter-wasms/out/tree-sitter-rust.wasm node_modules/tree-sitter-wasms/out/tree-sitter-go.wasm grammars/
scripts.build: npm run compile && npm run bundle && npm run copy-grammars
scripts.check: npm run compile && npm run lint && npm run test
